title: Federal Government Invests in Made-in-Canada Technologies to Advance Clean Fuels Sector
content: Canada announced over $11 million in funding under Natural Resources Canadaâ€™s Energy Innovation Program (EIP) and Clean Fuels Fund (CFF) to support clean fuel and industrial fuel-switching projects. The funding aims to advance low-carbon fuels in high-emitting sectors, boost biomass supply chains, and enhance domestic clean fuel production. Key projects include pilot production of sustainable diesel from wood waste (Quebec), development of plasma torches for heavy industry (Montreal), coal displacement in cement production (Ontario), and waste-to-SAF conversion (Alberta). Additional funding supports hydrogen feasibility studies, renewable fuel production from forestry waste, and biobased economic development in Manitoba.
author: Analysts Account
